meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Hidden tunnel discovered in Tijuana may have supported cross-border trafficking operations
items.summary: Authorities uncover a 265-meter underground tunnel near the U.S.-Mexico border equipped with lighting, ventilation and an electronic transport system.

items.title: Anti-cartel candidate 'The Tiger' channels Trump and Bukele in Colombia election shocker
items.summary: Analysts say Colombia's presidential election signals growing regional rejection of leftist governments as voters prioritize security and counternarcotics.

items.title: US military attacks Iran in 'self-defense strikes' over weekend
items.summary: CENTCOM announced that it targeted Iran in "self-defense strikes" during the weekend, asserting that CENTCOM will defend American interests.

items.title: Fireworks reportedly trigger mass horse stampede through Rome streets, injuring several soldiers
items.summary: Unauthorized fireworks reportedly triggered a mass horse stampede during a late-night rehearsal for Italy's Republic Day parade in Rome, injuring riders.

items.title: Hezbollah's 'game changing' night-hunting weapon punches through Israel's defenses: expert
items.summary: Drone expert warns Hezbollah's nighttime drone capabilities are game changing, using thermal sensors to target IDF troops under cover of darkness.

items.title: Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst
items.summary: Iran's supreme leader launched a sweeping counteroffensive against President Donald Trump, seeking to rally Middle Eastern nations into an anti-American alliance, an analyst says.

items.title: English cops cuffed teen stabbing victim after attacker claimed racial assault
items.summary: Police apologize after handcuffing stabbing victim Henry Nowak based on attacker's alleged racism claim, sparking outrage over British policing culture.

items.title: Anti-cartel hardliner channels Trump in bid to end Colombia's leftist era in pivotal election
items.summary: Colombia's next president could reshape U.S. counternarcotics cooperation as voters choose between security-first and dialogue-based approaches.

items.title: Pentagon hosts first-ever Israeli–Lebanese military talks aimed at curbing Hezbollah
items.summary: Israeli and Lebanese military delegations open U.S.-brokered talks at the Pentagon focused on ceasefire enforcement and border stability amid tensions.

items.title: Christian farming communities under siege as US report names Fulani militants Nigeria's deadliest threat
items.summary: The USCIRF report states that Fulani militant violence in Nigeria surpasses that of Boko Haram and ISIS, with mostly Christians and also some Muslims being targeted in widespread attacks.

items.title: Hamas struggles to fill leadership ranks as Israel hunts Oct 7 terrorists
items.summary: Hours after an Israeli airstrike killed Hamas military wing chief Mohammed Odeh, Gaza's Eid markets were full, revealing a disconnect with Hamas.

items.title: Drone strikes apartment building in NATO member Romania as Russia attacks neighboring Ukraine
items.summary: A drone entered Romanian airspace and struck an apartment building, causing an explosion and fire that injured multiple people, authorities said.

items.title: Moscow, Taliban forge military alliance in power grab after US Afghanistan exit: reports
items.summary: Russia and Afghanistan's Taliban government reportedly signed a military cooperation pact, solidifying Moscow's growing alliance in Central Asia.

items.title: Putin lands $16.5B nuclear win on Russia’s doorstep in massive Kazakhstan pact: reports
items.summary: Putin and Tokayev signed a landmark $16.5 billion nuclear power plant deal, marking Russia's geopolitical victory in Central Asia, reports say.

items.title: Israel accuses UN of placing it on same sexual violence blacklist as Hamas terrorists, severs ties
items.summary: Israeli officials blast the U.N. after accusing the body of placing Israel on a sexual violence blacklist that also includes Hamas and other terrorist groups.

items.title: Three wounded in Swiss train station 'bladed weapon' attack, phrase 'Allahu Akbar' allegedly shouted
items.summary: Police say a 31-year-old was arrested after allegedly wounding three people using a bladed weapon at train station in Switzerland.

items.title: US ally pledges support for Trump's push to break Iran's grip on Hormuz: 'We are ready to contribute'
items.summary: Czech Foreign Minister Macinka voices strong support for Trump, defends increased European defense spending and warns Iran poses a global nuclear threat.

items.title: Crash involving speeding train, minibus in Belgium leaves 4 dead including 2 children
items.summary: Four people, including two children, were killed when a passenger train hit a minibus at a crossing near Buggenhout, Belgium, during the morning rush.

items.title: Israel eliminates head of Hamas' military wing in Gaza strike
items.summary: Israeli forces killed Mohammed Odeh, the new head of Hamas' military wing, in an airstrike in northern Gaza, according to the Israel Defense Forces.

items.title: Iran and Houthi terror proxy facing Red Sea threat from pro-US African nation
items.summary: Sen. Ted Cruz advocates recognizing Somaliland as the U.S. reportedly explores military access to the breakaway state's port and airbase facilities.

items.title: Israeli ambassador compares France's far-left leader's rhetoric to Hitler as antisemitism surges
items.summary: France recorded 1,320 antisemitic acts in 2025, up from 436 in 2022, as Jewish families alter daily habits to avoid being identified, CRIF's president says.

items.title: Mother, boyfriend allegedly abandoned blindfolded young sons in remote forest as part of 'game': reports
items.summary: Marine Rousseau and Marc Ballabriga allegedly abandoned her two young children in a forest in Europe, blindfolding them as part of a so-called game.

items.title: ‘Designated target’ Mojtaba Khamenei to sign Trump deal in ‘unprecedented’ courier setup
items.summary: Experts say the U.S. is negotiating with an invisible counterparty as Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly remains in hiding and relies on secret couriers.

items.title: Funerals, beauty queens and bombs: The Ukrainian city that won’t let Putin win
items.summary: Mayor Sadovyi says Lviv has lost 2,000 citizens and built rehabilitation centers as the city tries to remain unbroken amid Russia's ongoing war.

items.title: World's humanitarian system buckling, 'no longer fit for purpose,' US-based researchers say
items.summary: A new Lancet report argues the global humanitarian system needs major reform as crises around the world overwhelm current aid delivery.
